steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank sits between 'known' and 'a nickname.' The phrase 'to be known by a nickname' uses the preposition 'by' to connect the passive verb to its object. No punctuation should separate 'by' from 'a nickname.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘by’: Plain preposition directly followed by its noun object — clean and grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘by—’: An em dash after 'by' would break the preposition from its object with no grammatical justification. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘by,’: A comma after 'by' wrongly separates the preposition from the noun phrase it governs. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘by:’: A colon introduces an independent clause or a list, not the object of a preposition. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
